game_name: Romance of the Three Kingdoms III: Dragon of Destiny

danger_signals.visual_form: map control visualization showing opposing faction domains
danger_signals.meaning: proximity to player-controlled territories indicates military threat

danger_signals.visual_form: faction ownership changes on territory map
danger_signals.meaning: loss of allied cities or emergence of new hostile powers

safety_signals.visual_form: contiguous map regions displaying unified color/faction identity
safety_signals.meaning: consolidated power base reduces vulnerability to fragmented attack

safety_signals.visual_form: neighboring territories controlled by allied faction
safety_signals.meaning: mutual defense and supply route protection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: historical warlord insignia (inferred from Romance of the Three Kingdoms novel tradition)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: city ownership color; character roster displays faction affiliation
territorial domain on map; character unit colors in non-combat interface

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: player-assigned warlord or custom character identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player cities marked distinctly; custom character portrait in roster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: centralized power concentration with warlord factions emerging as challenge to authority
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI warlords compete for territorial dominance; initial player advantage varies by chosen faction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: fragmented map showing multiple competing factions with balanced territorial distribution
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: high volatility in allegiances; multiple simultaneous conflicts visible on territory map

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Liu Bei seeks shelter in Xinye (AD 201)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Liu Bei faction isolated in specific territory; other warlords control majority of map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: asymmetric power dynamics; underdog faction positioning creates defensive gameplay incentives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: three major power blocs visible on map with distinct territorial boundaries
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: shift toward consolidated factions; reduced number of active warlords; clearer strategic alliances

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: three dominant factions control approximately equal territory distribution
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: stable three-way power balance; late-game territorial consolidation phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: succession scenario showing established power structure with inheritance-based leadership
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: late-game starting position; requires conquest of remaining independent territories

environmental_storytelling.example: map distribution reflects historical Three Kingdoms novel positions; player choices diverge from novel path, creating dynamic alternate history
46 unique cities each associated with specific warlord traditions; conquered cities retain historical significance through character rosters
unique character availability in specific cities signals faction strength and leadership capability; recruitment reflects territorial control
six/seven historical scenarios present different political configurations; choice of scenario determines initial diplomatic landscape and power distribution

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: rapid territorial conquest visible as color expansion on map; increased military activity in interface
static territorial boundaries; focus on city development rather than conquest; reduced military mobilization
non-aggressive stance toward allied territories; joint operations against common enemies; shared supply routes
character portrait and stats display in non-combat interface; available for player faction assignment
character stats unavailable for recruitment; identified as opposing faction unit; cannot be retained if territory conquered

notes: Source material provides game structure and scenario framework but limited explicit visual communication details. Manifestation patterns inferred from turn-based strategy paradigm and documented gameplay mechanics. Six core scenarios plus one additional PC-Engine scenario create distinct world-state presentations. Character statistics (War Ability, Intelligence, Politics, Charisma, Army Command, Naval Command) likely displayed in UI and communicate faction leadership depth. Political situations communicated primarily through map-based territorial control visualization and warlord availability lists rather than dynamic visual storytelling elements. Game does not follow novel narrative after startup, indicating branching world-states dependent on player choice and AI behavior.
sources_used: wikipedia - Romance of the Three Kingdoms III: Dragon of Destiny
